metadata.seed: Yeah, there's, like, my main friends that. Yeah, they kind of. It's like peer pressure, sort of. But I can't say no. I feel like I have to say yes just. Just because they are my friends. I do care about them and I don't want to lose them. But sometimes some of the stuff that we do, I feel like I don't really want to do it. And to keep them, I feel like I need to make the sacrifice to do what they want to do and go along with it, even though I know it might not be right. So I just kind of want to build up my self esteem and my confidence and accepting myself. That way I can tell them no and they won't overreact. So I want to be able to say it in a good way. That way I can keep them.

patient.query: Yeah, there's, like, my main friends that. Yeah, they kind of. It's like peer pressure, sort of. But I can't say no. I feel like I have to say yes just. Just because they are my friends. I do care about them and I don't want to lose them. But sometimes some of the stuff that we do, I feel like I don't really want to do it. And to keep them, I feel like I need to make the sacrifice to do what they want to do and go along with it, even though I know it might not be right. So I just kind of want to build up my self esteem and my confidence and accepting myself. That way I can tell them no and they won't overreact. So I want to be able to say it in a good way. That way I can keep them.

automatic_thoughts: I can't say no
I have to say yes
I need to make sacrifices
I don't want to lose my friends
I might not be right

neutral: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)
Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
It sounds like you're feeling caught between wanting to maintain your friendships and protect your own desires. When you're faced with pressure from your friends, what thoughts come up for you about saying no or making sacrifices?

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I often think that if I say no, they'll be disappointed or think I'm not a good friend. It's like, I want to be there for them, but I also feel this weight, like I'm losing a part of myself when I always put their needs first. Sometimes I just feel so... invisible, I guess?

automatic_thoughts: if I say no, they'll be disappointed
they'll think I'm not a good friend
I want to be there for them
I'm losing a part of myself when I always put their needs first

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to always meet others' needs, which might be why you feel that weight. That need to be there for people could be taking a toll on your sense of self. What do you think happens when you stop trying to always be available?

patient.query: When I think about stepping back a little, I get this rush of anxiety. Like, what if they're upset with me or think I don't care? But at the same time, I can feel that little voice inside saying it might be nice to have some space for myself. It’s confusing…I know it would feel good, but then there's this fear that creeps in.

automatic_thoughts: what if they're upset with me
what if they think I don't care
it might be nice to have some space for myself

neutral: Fear of being left alone during period of dependence (finding)

llm_response.response: When you say that, I hear a lot of confusion about what might be happening. Can you tell me more about what you notice when these thoughts come up?

patient.query: Yeah, it's like my mind goes a bit blank, but I can feel my heart racing. I start to think about all the times I've let myself down because I didn't speak up or say what I really wanted. It’s frustrating, you know? I want to be honest, but there's this panic about disappointing others. I find myself just going along with whatever they want, even when I'm uncomfortable.

automatic_thoughts: I've let myself down
I didn't speak up
I want to be honest
I have to go along with others
behaviors: going along with whatever they want

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: "It sounds like your body is reacting to the pressure you're feeling, and those thoughts about past failures are really weighing on you. When you mention feeling frustrated and panicked, it makes me wonder if there's a specific thought pattern that keeps coming up when this happens?"

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it does feel like there's this heavy cloud of pressure every time I think about saying what I really want. I often remember times when I didn’t stand up for myself and then replay those moments in my head, wishing I had done something different. It’s like I can't shake off that feeling of not being good enough, like I keep messing things up. And then it just spirals into this big ball of anxiety.

triggers: thinking about saying what I really want
remembering times I didn’t stand up for myself
automatic_thoughts: wishing I had done something different
I can't shake off that feeling of not being good enough
I keep messing things up

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like the pressure you're feeling is really weighing on you, especially when you think about speaking up for yourself. And it makes sense that replaying those past moments would bring up anxiety, since they seem to be stuck in your mind.

patient.query: Yeah, it feels like I'm constantly battling with those memories, you know? Like I'm stuck in this loop, and it just adds to the heaviness. I want to be more confident and stand up for myself, but then I worry about how it will affect my relationships. It's like I'm caught between wanting to be true to myself and this fear that it could push people away.

automatic_thoughts: I'm stuck in a loop
I want to be more confident
Being true to myself could push people away

llm_response.response: It sounds like those memories and relationship concerns are creating a heavy emotional burden for you. The feeling of being stuck in a loop seems to be weighing you down, and I can sense the tension between wanting to be authentic and fearing the outcome.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is heavy. Sometimes I feel like I'm just trying to keep everyone else happy, and in the process, I lose sight of what makes me happy. I wonder if people even see me for who I am or just what I do for them. It's exhausting, but then when I think about trying to change, I get scared of what that means for those connections. It's hard to find a balance.

triggers: trying to keep everyone else happy
automatic_thoughts: I lose sight of what makes me happy
people don't see me for who I am
changing will affect my connections

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response:
**FINAL RESPONSE**  
"Thank you for sharing that. It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to meet others' expectations, and it's understandable how that might feel draining. At the same time, the thought of changing things could bring up worries about how it might affect your relationships. How does that tension show up in your daily life?"

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely shows up in little ways, like when I hesitate to make plans or when I feel guilty for wanting some time to myself. I often end up doing things I don’t really want to do just to avoid conflict or make sure everyone else is happy. It feels like I’m always on this tightrope, trying not to fall into disappointment or anger from others. I guess that constant balancing act is just... tiring.

triggers: feeling guilty for wanting time alone
automatic_thoughts: I have to please everyone
I can't disappoint others
Taking time for myself is selfish
behaviors: doing things I don’t want to do

entailment: Tends to turn anger against self (finding)
neutral: Able to recognize own emotional pain and distress (finding)

llm_response.response:
**FINAL RESPONSE:**  
It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure to meet others' expectations, and that might be contributing to the exhaustion you feel. When you say you end up doing things you don’t want to do, what’s the cost to you in the moment?

patient.query: In the moment, it just feels like I’m pushing my own needs aside, and that kind of hurts, you know? I might smile and go along with it, but inside I’m feeling really frustrated and sometimes even a little resentful. It’s like I’m agreeing to things I don’t care about, which just leaves me feeling even more disconnected from myself. I guess I’m losing bits of who I am every time I do that.

automatic_thoughts: I'm pushing my own needs aside
I'm agreeing to things I don't care about
I'm losing bits of who I am
behaviors: smile and go along with it

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of internal conflict between outward compliance and inner resentment. When you're pushing your own needs aside, it must feel like you're losing parts of yourself, which could explain the hurt and disconnection you're feeling.

patient.query: Yeah, that resonates with me. It’s tough because I don’t want to come off as selfish, but I also feel that sense of loss whenever I silence my own voice. I think sometimes I just want to be seen and heard for who I am, not just what I can do for others. It’s like a tug-of-war inside me, and I’m just left feeling... empty, I guess.

automatic_thoughts: I don’t want to come off as selfish
I want to be seen and heard for who I am
I feel a sense of loss when I silence my own voice

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It makes sense that you'd feel that internal conflict. There's a tension between wanting to be considerate and honoring your own needs, and that can definitely create emotional emptiness. It sounds like you're carrying a lot of that weight internally.
